description: Agent coordination manifest - claims prevent conflicts

changes: Add warmup_repeats parameter to run_index_and_relevance_benchmark for more accurate timing
Add clear_cache parameter to run_index_and_relevance_benchmark to test cold-start performance
Add memory delta tracking (index_memory_delta_mb, query_memory_delta_mb)
Add query_stddev_ms for measuring timing consistency
Add run_multi_query_benchmark for batch query benchmarking
Extend compare_benchmark_to_baseline to check max_query_stddev_ms
benchmarking_improvements: Warmup runs eliminate JIT/cache effects for more accurate measurements
Memory delta tracking reveals index/query memory footprint
Multi-query benchmark enables batch testing across multiple search scenarios

changes: Add incremental snapshots with file hash tracking
Cache snapshot index with LRU mechanism
Parallel file hashing for large snapshots
Add content_hash field to Snapshot dataclass
Add reference_snapshot field for incremental snapshots
Optimize _collect_files_for_snapshot with pre-filtering
snapshot_improvements: Incremental snapshots avoid re-archiving unchanged files
Index caching reduces repeated JSON parsing
Parallel hashing speeds up change detection
Content hash enables dedupe across snapshots

changes: Move module-level imports out of hot functions
Pre-compile regex patterns at module load time
Remove redundant list() copies in cache returns
Optimize sensitive_path_reason_optimized with LRU-style caching
performance_notes: Import inside function adds ~4% overhead per call

changes: Added _find_fork_bomb_unescaped() for escaped fork bomb detection
Extended _DANGEROUS_ENV_VARS to include dyld_library_path
Enhanced _blocked_curl_wget() for silent fetch detection
Fixed dangerous env var detection for PATH= anywhere
Added _blocked_sudo_n() to block sudo -n and sudo -E
security_notes: Fork bomb bypass via escapes now detected

changes: Added session_start/session_end event logging
Added agent_id parameter to log_tool_call()
Added policy_change audit event type
Added ensure_session_start_logged()
audit_improvements: Session lifecycle now has explicit audit events

changes: Added success_criteria_json parameter to create_plan()
Added validate_plan() function for plan validation
Added critique_plan() function for plan scoring
Added TypedDict classes PlanValidationResult and PlanCritiqueResult
Added relevance scoring in explore_approaches()
Added _score_keyword_match() helper
Added include_low_relevance filter parameter
planning_improvements: Plans now support explicit success criteria
validate_plan() checks goal length, step descriptions, success criteria
critique_plan() provides 0-100 scoring with issues and suggestions
Approach exploration uses weighted scoring (70% relevance, 30% complexity)

changes: Add error_handling criterion
Add imports criterion
Add type_coverage criterion
Added _SYNONYM_MAP for keyword variant detection
Added _INTENT_PATTERNS for regex-based intent detection
Added _STORE_DIR_CACHE with invalidate_store_dir_cache() for caching
Enhanced _detect_keywords() to use synonyms and intent patterns
Added count validation (0 < count <= 20) to prevent infinite loops
Added relevance scoring with _score_keyword_match()
Added include_low_relevance parameter to explore_approaches()
Added TestSynonymDetection test class
Added TestIntentPatternDetection test class
Added TestEdgeCases test class for boundary conditions
explorer_improvements: Synonym detection: sorting->sort, find->search, performance->optimize, etc.
Store dir caching reduces repeated filesystem calls
Relevance scoring ranks approaches by keyword frequency

agent-31-memory.task: memory persistence improvements and cleanup strategies
changes: Added cleanup_lessons() method with TTL-based expiration and max-lessons overflow handling
Added clear_all() method to wipe memory files
Added backup() method with backup file support
Added DEFAULT_MAX_LESSONS (500) and DEFAULT_LESSON_TTL_DAYS (90) constants
cleanup_improvements: TTL-based expiration removes lessons older than configurable days
Overflow cleanup keeps highest-hit lessons when exceeding max
Full wipe capability for privacy/reset scenarios

changes: Added get_rate() method to MetricsCollector for rate calculations
Added get_percentile() and get_histogram_stats() for detailed histogram analysis
Added percentile render output (_p50, _p95, _p99) in render_prometheus()
Added _last_update timestamp for tracking metrics freshness
Added agent_id, operation_type, cache_hit fields to SpanAttributes
Added in-memory span storage with get_recent_spans() and get_span_stats()
Added set_level() method for dynamic tracing level control
Span storage limited to 1000 entries to prevent memory bloat
observability_improvements: Metrics now track rate per second, not just counts
Prometheus output includes percentiles for latency analysis
Tracing spans stored locally for performance analysis
Span stats provide error rates and duration percentiles
Dynamic tracing level control for debugging
actionable_metrics: tool_call_rate: calls per second by tool
latency_p95: 95th percentile latency for SLA monitoring
cache_hit_rate: cache efficiency from trace attributes

agent-29-dashboard.task: dashboard UI improvements and metrics display
changes: Added metrics row with total tasks and status breakdown
Added color-coded status dots for visual clarity
Improved empty states with descriptive messages
Added responsive grid layout for mobile devices
Added dark mode color variables for new status colors
dashboard_improvements: Metrics display shows task counts by status with colored indicators
Status dots provide quick visual scanning of task states
Empty states now show contextual messages instead of generic text
Mobile layout uses responsive grid with 2-column metrics on smaller screens

changes: Added compact, benchmark, platform to SUPPORTED_WORKFLOW_MODES for consistency
Added missing PROMPT_ARGUMENTS entries for compact, benchmark, platform workflows
Added missing PROMPT_FOCUS_ARG mappings for compact, benchmark, platform
Added compact, benchmark, platform to WORKFLOW_STEPS
Added compact, benchmark, platform to WORKFLOW_EXAMPLES
Added missing compact, benchmark, platform to WORKFLOW_DISCOVERY_TERMS
Added missing compact, benchmark, platform to WORKFLOW_DEFAULT_FOCUS
Added compact, benchmark, platform to WORKFLOW_PROMPT_TEMPLATES
Improved optimize template with concrete low-risk improvement guidance
Improved debug template with hypothesis-driven approach
Added ToolMetadata class with name, description, version, read_only, destructive, open_world, tags
Added version and tags parameters to ToolRegistrationContext.register()
Added default_version configurable on ToolRegistrationContext
Added get_metadata() and get_all_metadata() methods for discovery
Added tool_version and tool_tags parameters to register_smart_tools()
tool_improvements: ToolMetadata provides structured metadata for each registered tool
Discovery methods allow runtime tool introspection

agent-24-shell-analysis.task: shell command parsing and analysis improvements
changes: Add _parse_error_details() for informative parse error messages with position
Add compound command analysis (&&, ||, ;) for chained risky operations
Add _extract_git_subcommand() helper to replace duplicated git parsing logic
Add dangerous glob pattern detection in rm/cd/mkdir commands
analysis_improvements: Parse errors now include character position and problematic character
Compound commands with && and || are flagged as high risk around risky operations
Git subcommand extraction refactored to avoid code duplication
Glob patterns (*, ?, []) in dangerous commands detected as potential risk

agent-28-onboarding.task: onboarding flow and doctor diagnostics improvement
changes: Added quick_command field to onboarding tips for first-run quick wins
Added fix_suggestion field to DoctorCheck dataclass
Added quick_fixes tuple to DoctorReport for actionable fixes
Added _update_check helper to consolidate check building
Added quick_fixes aggregation from failed checks
improvements: Onboarding tips now include quick_command for immediate user wins
Doctor checks now provide fix_suggestion when check fails
DoctorReport exposes quick_fixes tuple for tooling integration
Security doctor report uses same helper for consistency

agent-35-config.task: configuration validation and environment variable handling improvements
changes: Add _ENV_PREFIX constant for consistent env var prefix handling
Add validate_config_value() for centralized config validation
Add load_config_from_toml() for TOML file loading support
Add _validate_all_config() for comprehensive config validation on load
Enhance configure_from_env_state() to use _ENV_PREFIX constant
config_improvements: Centralized validation function for all config keys
Fail-fast validation on config load prevents invalid states
Consistent env var prefix for maintainability

changes: Added delete_many() to distributed_cache.py for batch deletion
Added get_many_bulk() using Redis MGET for parallel fetching
Added set_many_pipeline() using Redis pipeline for batch writes
Added cache hit/miss statistics tracking to distributed_cache.py
Added TTL-based invalidation to workflow_cache.py
Added invalidate_pattern() for selective cache invalidation
Added clear_disk_cache() to workflow_cache.py
caching_improvements: Batch operations now use Redis MGET/pipeline instead of N sequential calls
Cache statistics track hits, misses, and eviction count
Workflow cache entries now have TTL and pattern-based invalidation
Disk cache can now be explicitly cleared

agent-34-api.task: API consistency and type hint improvements
changes: Added ToolImplProtocol for consistent tool implementation interface
Added ToolResultTypedDict for structured tool responses
Added AuditCallbackProtocol for type-safe audit callbacks
Improved type hints on ToolRegistrationContext methods
Replaced loose Any types with proper Callable/Protocol types
api_improvements: Tool implementations now have consistent async signature
Audit callbacks have explicit parameter types
ToolResults uses TypedDict for better IDE support

changes: Added jitter to RetryConfig for exponential backoff randomization
Added total_timeout to RetryConfig for absolute retry timeout
Added on_retry callback to RetryConfig for logging/metrics hooks
Added CircuitOpenError exception class for circuit breaker errors
Added excluded_exceptions to CircuitBreakerConfig
Added on_state_change callback to CircuitBreakerConfig
Added fallback_func parameter to CircuitBreaker.call()
Added _transition_to() helper for state change callbacks
Added total_calls, total_failures, last_success_time tracking
Added is_closed, is_open, is_half_open properties
improvements: Jitter prevents thundering herd on retry storms
on_state_change enables audit trail for state transitions
State properties provide quick state checks

changes: Extended _SENSITIVE_KEYS with: private_key, access_token, auth_token, session_id, credentials, credential, refresh_token, client_secret, aws_access_key, aws_secret_key
Added content patterns for bearer tokens, JWT tokens (eyJ...), AWS access keys (AKIA...), AWS secret keys
Added content patterns for private key PEM headers
Added content patterns for UUID tokens, GitHub tokens (ghp_...), GitLab tokens (glpat-...), Slack tokens (xox...)
redaction_improvements: No false negatives: all common token formats now detected
JWT tokens now caught by content pattern
AWS access keys (AKIA...) now explicitly detected

changes: Added _MAX_REFLECT_RECURSION_DEPTH=3 and _MAX_META_REVIEW_ITEMS=20 constants to prevent infinite loops
Added _build_reflection_summary() for analyzing tool call patterns with depth tracking
Added reflect_on_recent_work tool: analyzes recent tool calls, computes tool counts, error rates, latency stats, and provides actionable suggestions
Added meta_review tool: reviews multiple tool results for inconsistencies between ok status and error fields
Added agent_introspection tool: exposes reflective tools and meta-tools to agents for self-awareness
reflective_features: reflect_on_recent_work analyzes patterns in recent tool calls with bounded recursion
meta_review checks result consistency with truncation protection
agent_introspection provides self-awareness about available reflective capabilities
safety_measures: Both tools are read_only and non-destructive
